channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Sigrid Jusélius Foundation Grants 2026: Major Funding Opportunities for Medical Researchers at Every Career Stage
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Funding is one of the most critical factors enabling groundbreaking scientific discoveries and sustaining long-term research careers. For decades, the Sigrid Jusélius Foundation has been one of the most influential supporters of medical research in Finland, providing significant funding opportunities to researchers working at various stages of their academic and professional journeys. Through its diverse […]

The post Sigrid Jusélius Foundation Grants 2026: Major Funding Opportunities for Medical Researchers at Every Career Stage appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Funding is one of the most critical factors enabling groundbreaking scientific discoveries and sustaining long-term research careers. For decades, the Sigrid Jusélius Foundation has been one of the most influential supporters of medical research in Finland, providing significant funding opportunities to researchers working at various stages of their academic and professional journeys.

Through its diverse portfolio of grants, fellowships, and research positions, the foundation aims to promote high-quality medical research, strengthen research careers, and advance scientific knowledge that contributes to better healthcare outcomes.

The foundation primarily supports research conducted in Finland, while also encouraging international collaboration by funding research placements abroad and inviting foreign scholars to Finnish institutions.

Notably, at least 75 percent of the grants awarded by the foundation go to Finnish researchers, reinforcing the country’s research ecosystem while also facilitating global partnerships.

Several funding opportunities are currently open for applications in 2026, particularly for researchers seeking fellowships or senior research placements abroad.

About the Sigrid Jusélius Foundation

The Sigrid Jusélius Foundation is dedicated to supporting top-level medical research in Finland. Its funding programs are designed to promote scientific excellence, enhance international collaboration, and support researchers throughout their careers—from postdoctoral researchers to senior academics.

By providing financial support for research projects, fellowships, and research positions, the foundation enables scientists to pursue innovative research initiatives that may lead to major advancements in medicine and public health.

In addition to funding research careers, the foundation also supports medical symposiums, providing platforms for scientific exchange, collaboration, and dissemination of new knowledge.

Available Grant Opportunities

The foundation offers several types of funding opportunities tailored to different stages of the research career. These grants support both research conducted in Finland and research placements abroad, helping researchers expand their expertise and collaborate internationally.

Below are the primary funding opportunities available through the foundation.

Fellowship (1–3 Years)

The Fellowship is designed for researchers who have completed their doctoral studies and wish to pursue advanced medical research abroad.

This fellowship enables early-career researchers to develop their expertise, strengthen international collaboration, and contribute to innovative research projects at leading research institutions worldwide.

Key Details

  • Duration: 1–3 years

  • Target group: Researchers who have defended their doctoral thesis

  • Purpose: Medical research abroad

Application Period

15 March – 15 April 2026

This opportunity is particularly valuable for postdoctoral researchers seeking to expand their international research networks and gain experience in new research environments.

Senior Fellowship (6–12 Months)

The Senior Fellowship supports experienced researchers who wish to conduct advanced medical research abroad for a shorter duration.

This funding allows senior-level researchers to collaborate with international research institutions, contribute to high-impact projects, and strengthen international partnerships.

Key Details

  • Duration: 6–12 months

  • Target group: Senior-level researchers

  • Purpose: Medical research abroad

Application Period

15 March – 15 April 2026

The Senior Fellowship is ideal for established researchers seeking to deepen their expertise or contribute to collaborative global research initiatives.

Visiting Professor Grant (3–12 Months)

The Visiting Professor Grant supports foreign senior researchers who wish to work at Finnish research institutes.

This initiative encourages knowledge exchange between international scholars and Finnish research institutions, strengthening Finland’s position as a hub for cutting-edge medical research.

Key Details

  • Duration: 3–12 months

  • Target group: Foreign senior researchers

  • Purpose: Conduct research at Finnish institutions

Application Period

1 November – 30 November 2026

1–3 Year Research Grants

These grants are designed to support research projects led by experienced scientists and emerging research leaders.

Key Details

  • Duration: 1–3 years

  • Target group:

    • Senior researchers

    • Young group leaders

Application Period

1 November – 30 November 2026

These grants provide funding that allows researchers to pursue long-term medical research initiatives that contribute to scientific innovation.

Large Research Grant (3–5 Years)

The Large Research Grant is intended for highly accomplished academic researchers who lead major research initiatives.

Target Group

  • Professors

  • Highly merited adjunct professors (docents)

This funding supports larger-scale research projects with significant scientific impact.

Senior Researcher Position (4 Years)

The Senior Researcher Grant supports independent medical researchers who have already established themselves in their field.

Key Details

  • Duration: 4 years

  • Target group:

    • Adjunct professors (docents)

    • Independent medical researchers with equivalent qualifications

Application Period

15 September – 15 October 2026

Medical Professorship (5 Years)

The foundation also provides funding to support medical professorships, helping universities and research institutions maintain world-class academic leadership in medical science.

Key Details

  • Duration: 5 years

  • Purpose: Financing medical professorship positions

Medical Symposium Funding

In addition to research grants and fellowships, the Sigrid Jusélius Foundation also supports medical symposiums that promote knowledge exchange among researchers.

These symposium grants are awarded every two years.

Application Period

15 September – 15 October 2025

Application Process

All grant applications must be submitted through the foundation’s electronic application system during the designated application periods.

Applicants should carefully review the specific instructions provided on each grant page before submitting their proposal.

In most cases, researchers can receive only one grant at a time, ensuring fair distribution of funding opportunities among applicants.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their research proposals well in advance to meet the application deadlines.

Annual Grant Application Timeline

The Sigrid Jusélius Foundation organizes its funding opportunities throughout the year according to a structured annual application cycle.

Key funding rounds occur in:

  • March–April (Fellowships and Senior Fellowships)

  • September–October (Senior Researcher Grants and Symposium Funding)

  • November (Visiting Professor Grants and 1–3 Year Research Grants)

This structured timeline allows researchers to plan their applications according to their career stage and research needs.

Why Apply for Sigrid Jusélius Foundation Funding?

Receiving a grant from the Sigrid Jusélius Foundation is widely recognized as a prestigious achievement within the medical research community.

The foundation’s grants provide:

  • Significant financial support for high-quality research

  • Opportunities for international collaboration

  • Career advancement for emerging and established researchers

  • Access to leading research institutions worldwide

Through these grants, the foundation continues to strengthen Finland’s role in global medical research while fostering scientific innovation that benefits society.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Sigrid Jusélius Foundation Grants 2026: Major Funding Opportunities for Medical Researchers at Every Career Stage appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Leadership Program 2026–2027: Empower Your Community and Become a Changemaker in Malawi
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Young leaders are key drivers of sustainable development, innovation, and community transformation. Across Africa, youth-led initiatives are increasingly shaping economic opportunities, strengthening entrepreneurship ecosystems, and supporting inclusive community development. Recognizing the powerful role young people play in shaping the future, Rays of Hope, in partnership with Digital Opportunity Trust (DOT) and the Mastercard Foundation, has […]

The post Youth Leadership Program 2026–2027: Empower Your Community and Become a Changemaker in Malawi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young leaders are key drivers of sustainable development, innovation, and community transformation. Across Africa, youth-led initiatives are increasingly shaping economic opportunities, strengthening entrepreneurship ecosystems, and supporting inclusive community development. Recognizing the powerful role young people play in shaping the future, Rays of Hope, in partnership with Digital Opportunity Trust (DOT) and the Mastercard Foundation, has launched the Youth Leadership Program 2026–2027.

This initiative, part of the Going Beyond – Partnering for a Youth-Led Future Project, is designed to empower young graduates with leadership, digital, and entrepreneurial skills so they can drive meaningful social and economic change within their communities.

The program offers an immersive experience combining training, mentorship, and community engagement. Selected participants will receive comprehensive leadership training and then return to their communities to support local entrepreneurs and small businesses, particularly women-led enterprises.

With a focus on peer-to-peer learning, entrepreneurship, and digital inclusion, the Youth Leadership Program aims to equip young people with the tools needed to strengthen local economies while building their own career opportunities.

Application Deadline: 24 March 2026

About the Youth Leadership Program

The Youth Leadership Program is a structured initiative designed to transform young graduates into community facilitators, coaches, and social impact leaders. Through intensive training and community deployment, participants will gain practical skills that prepare them to support local economic development while strengthening their leadership capacity.

Participants will undergo three weeks of intensive training, followed by a six-month community placement where they will apply their newly acquired knowledge to empower their peers and local entrepreneurs.

The program specifically focuses on strengthening micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs), with a strong emphasis on supporting women entrepreneurs, who represent 70% of the program’s target beneficiaries.

By equipping youth with the ability to share digital skills, business knowledge, and leadership strategies, the initiative creates a ripple effect that strengthens entire communities.

The Going Beyond Project

The Youth Leadership Program is implemented under the Going Beyond – Partnering for a Youth-Led Future Project, a major initiative led by Digital Opportunity Trust in collaboration with the Mastercard Foundation.

The project aims to scale DOT’s evidence-based peer-to-peer youth leadership model, enabling young leaders to deliver programs that foster entrepreneurship and digital inclusion in their communities.

The initiative will operate across four African countries:

  • Côte d’Ivoire

  • Malawi

  • Zambia

  • Tanzania

Over a five-year period, the project will expand youth-led leadership initiatives across the continent and gradually transfer the responsibility for delivering programs directly to young leaders themselves.

By investing in youth leadership, the project aims to create a sustainable network of empowered young professionals capable of driving economic development and community innovation.

Program Structure

The Youth Leadership Program is designed as a multi-phase learning and deployment experience that combines training, mentorship, and hands-on community work.

Phase 1: Leadership and Skills Training

Selected participants will attend an intensive three-week training program where they will develop key competencies required for their leadership role.

Training topics include:

  • Leadership and community facilitation

  • Digital skills and digital entrepreneurship

  • Basic business development skills

  • Communication and coaching techniques

  • Self-confidence and professional development

  • Career development strategies

The training prepares participants to act as program facilitators, mentors, and community changemakers.

Phase 2: Community Deployment

After completing the training, youth leaders will return to their communities and begin a six-month deployment where they will work directly with local entrepreneurs and young people.

During this phase, youth leaders will:

  • Deliver training sessions to peers and entrepreneurs

  • Provide coaching and mentorship to small business owners

  • Promote digital literacy and business skills

  • Encourage innovation and entrepreneurship within their communities

This hands-on phase ensures that participants apply their learning in real-world settings while generating tangible impact.

Key Benefits of the Youth Leadership Program

The Youth Leadership Program provides participants with numerous professional and personal development opportunities.

Comprehensive Leadership Training

Participants receive structured training that strengthens their leadership abilities, entrepreneurial mindset, and digital competencies.

Career Development Opportunities

The program includes career development support and employment linkages that help participants expand their professional opportunities.

Community Impact

Youth leaders will directly support the growth of local MSMEs by sharing practical knowledge and tools that improve business sustainability and growth.

Membership in a Pan-African Youth Network

Participants will join the DOT Youth Community, a vibrant network of more than 5,000 youth leaders across Africa.

This network provides access to future collaborations, mentorship opportunities, and international programs.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet several eligibility requirements to participate in the Youth Leadership Program.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be between 18 and 35 years old.

Nationality or Residency

Candidates must be Malawian nationals or legal residents of Malawi.

Educational Background

Applicants must be recent graduates from accredited universities or colleges and possess one of the following:

  • Certificate

  • Diploma

  • Bachelor’s degree

Graduates from credible Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) programs may also be eligible.

Applicants must have graduated within the last six months to five years.

Location Requirement

Applicants must currently live in one of the following communities:

  • Ndirande

  • Bangwe

  • Machinjiri

  • Chilomoni

  • Chileka

  • Kabula

  • Lunzu

  • Mpemba

  • Zingwangwa

  • Limbe

Availability

Applicants must be fully available for the eight-month program duration, from May 2026 to December 2026.

Skills and Equipment

Applicants must:

  • Have access to a smartphone or laptop

  • Possess basic computer skills

  • Communicate fluently in the local language

  • Demonstrate strong leadership potential and commitment to community development

Employment Status

Applicants should be unemployed or underemployed at the time of application.

No prior professional experience is required.

Inclusion and Equal Opportunity

The Going Beyond Project is committed to promoting inclusion and equal opportunity for young people.

The program strongly encourages applications from:

  • Young women

  • Youth with disabilities

  • Young people from rural communities

  • Youth living in poverty

  • Displaced young people

To support participants with childcare responsibilities, the program will also provide on-site childcare services for those who demonstrate a need.

Recruitment TimelineThe recruitment and deployment process will follow the timeline below:

  • Call for Applications: 9 March 2026

  • Application Deadline: 24 March 2026

  • Announcement of Finalists: April 2026

  • Induction and Training of Trainers: May 2026

  • Contracting and Official Deployment: May 2026

  • Placement Start Date: May 2026

  • Placement End Date: December 2026

Application Requirements

Applicants must prepare the following documents before starting the online application process:

  • Scanned copy of National ID

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV) or resume

  • Scanned copy of higher education certificate

  • Cover letter

Selection Process

The selection process consists of three stages designed to identify committed and motivated youth leaders.

Stage One

Selected applicants will participate in the three-week training program.

Stage Two

Participants will then be selected for the six-month community deployment, where they will serve as Youth Leaders.

Stage Three

Youth Leaders will receive ongoing career development training and mentorship from Rays of Hope and its local and international partners.

The program aims to ensure a fair and accessible selection process. Applicants requiring accommodations during interviews or assessments are encouraged to contact the organizers.

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Youth Leadership Program 2026–2027: Empower Your Community and Become a Changemaker in Malawi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Facilitator Opportunity on Gender Equality and Women’s Participation: ASEF Opens Call for Capacity-Building Programme (2026)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
In an era where inclusive governance and sustainable development are central to global progress, ensuring meaningful women’s participation in leadership and decision-making has never been more critical. Despite decades of advocacy and international commitments, women continue to face structural barriers that limit their representation and influence across political, social, and economic spheres. To address these […]

The post Facilitator Opportunity on Gender Equality and Women’s Participation: ASEF Opens Call for Capacity-Building Programme (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In an era where inclusive governance and sustainable development are central to global progress, ensuring meaningful women’s participation in leadership and decision-making has never been more critical. Despite decades of advocacy and international commitments, women continue to face structural barriers that limit their representation and influence across political, social, and economic spheres.

To address these challenges and strengthen cross-regional collaboration, the Asia-Europe Foundation (ASEF) has announced an open call for an experienced Individual Consultant / Facilitator to design and deliver a high-level capacity-building programme on gender equality and women’s participation. The initiative aims to bring together participants from Asia and Europe to enhance their skills, knowledge, and practical tools to promote women’s leadership and gender-inclusive policymaking.

The programme will run during March–April 2026, with a training event planned for September 2026 in Kunming, Yunnan, China. This opportunity is designed for professionals with extensive expertise in gender equality, governance, and training facilitation who are committed to advancing inclusive development and strengthening women’s leadership across sectors.

Why Women’s Participation Matters for Sustainable Development

Women’s full participation in decision-making is essential for achieving the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, particularly Sustainable Development Goal 5 (Gender Equality). Inclusive societies require governance systems that reflect the diversity of their populations and provide equal opportunities for participation.

However, progress toward gender equality remains uneven worldwide. Women often face systemic challenges such as:

  • Limited access to political leadership and decision-making roles

  • Structural barriers within institutions and governance frameworks

  • Insufficient gender-responsive budgeting and policy planning

  • Lack of gender-disaggregated data for evidence-based policymaking

  • Weak monitoring and evaluation mechanisms for gender equality initiatives

These persistent gaps make targeted capacity-building initiatives essential for empowering leaders and practitioners to integrate gender equality into policies, programs, and institutional frameworks.

The upcoming ASEF training programme is designed to address these gaps by equipping participants with practical skills and tools to advance women’s participation across governance, advocacy, and community development sectors.

Overview of the Capacity-Building Programme

ASEF’s three-day programme will bring together 20–25 participants from Asia and Europe representing diverse professional backgrounds, including government institutions, civil society organizations, academia, and the private sector.

The training aims to provide participants with practical and actionable strategies to strengthen women’s participation in leadership and policymaking processes.

Key Objectives of the Programme

The programme is designed to achieve several strategic goals:

1. Strengthening Gender Mainstreaming in Policy and Programming

Participants will learn how to integrate gender equality principles into policymaking, development programming, advocacy initiatives, and grassroots community actions.

2. Enhancing Women’s Leadership Across Sectors

The programme will explore strategies for promoting women’s participation and leadership across governance systems, civil society structures, and institutional environments.

3. Building Technical Skills for Gender-Responsive Governance

Participants will gain hands-on knowledge in areas such as:

  • Gender-sensitive data collection and analysis

  • Gender-responsive budgeting frameworks

  • Monitoring and evaluation systems for gender equality initiatives

4. Advancing Sustainable Development Goals

The training will highlight the cross-cutting importance of women’s participation for achieving multiple Sustainable Development Goals beyond gender equality, including:

  • Climate action

  • Peace and security

  • Digital transformation

  • Inclusive governance

By connecting gender equality to broader global challenges, the programme aims to strengthen interdisciplinary collaboration and policy innovation.

Role of the Facilitator

The selected Facilitator will play a central role in shaping the learning experience and ensuring the programme delivers meaningful outcomes for participants.

Working closely with ASEF, the Facilitator will support both the design and implementation of the training programme.

Core Responsibilities

The Facilitator’s responsibilities will include several key tasks:

Programme Design

The Facilitator will collaborate with ASEF to develop the training curriculum, including:

  • Structuring the programme agenda

  • Identifying the appropriate target audience

  • Designing participatory learning methodologies

  • Developing training materials and resources

Coordination with Experts

The Facilitator will recommend and collaborate with subject-matter experts or co-trainers who can contribute specialized knowledge to the programme.

They will also facilitate two to three sessions during the three-day training event.

Assessment and Evaluation

To measure the programme’s effectiveness, the Facilitator will design and implement pre-training and post-training assessment tools that evaluate participant learning outcomes.

These evaluation tools will help identify knowledge gains, practical skill development, and areas for future improvement.

Reporting and Documentation

After the programme concludes, the Facilitator will prepare a concise training report summarizing:

  • The facilitation process

  • Key discussions and learning outcomes

  • Participant feedback and insights

  • Recommendations for future capacity-building initiatives

Follow-Up Engagement for Long-Term Impact

ASEF encourages Facilitators to propose follow-up initiatives that extend the impact of the training programme beyond the initial three-day workshop.

These follow-up activities may include:

  • Collaborative projects between participants

  • Policy advocacy initiatives

  • Knowledge-sharing networks

  • Continued mentorship or capacity-building programs

Such initiatives can help translate the training’s lessons into long-term institutional and community change.

Selection Criteria for the Facilitator

To ensure the success of the programme, ASEF is seeking candidates with strong professional expertise in gender equality and training facilitation.

Expertise in Gender Equality and Women’s Participation

Applicants should have 7 to 10 years of professional experience in areas such as:

  • Gender equality and women’s empowerment

  • Women’s leadership and participation in governance

  • Gender mainstreaming within sustainable development programmes

Candidates must also demonstrate strong knowledge of international frameworks related to gender equality, including:

  • The Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW)

  • The Beijing Platform for Action

  • The United Nations 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development

Training and Facilitation Experience

The selected Facilitator should have proven experience in:

  • Designing gender-related training programmes

  • Applying participatory and adult-learning methodologies

  • Facilitating discussions in English with diverse professional audiences

The ability to engage participants from different sectors and cultural backgrounds is essential.

Technical Expertise

Candidates should possess strong technical knowledge in areas such as:

  • Gender analysis methodologies

  • Gender-responsive budgeting

  • Use of gender-disaggregated data for policymaking

  • Monitoring and evaluation frameworks for gender equality initiatives

Regional and Cross-Cultural Experience

Experience working in ASEM member countries or other multicultural environments will be highly valued.

Applicants should also demonstrate the ability to integrate intersectional and inclusive perspectives into training design and facilitation.

Availability

The selected Facilitator must be available to travel to Kunming, Yunnan, China during the first half of September 2026 for the programme’s training event.

Proposal Submission Requirements

Interested candidates must submit a detailed proposal outlining their approach to designing and facilitating the training programme.

The proposal should not exceed four pages and must include the following components:

  • Name and affiliation of the Facilitator

  • Names and affiliations of proposed expert trainers or co-facilitators

  • Objectives and expected learning outcomes of the training

  • Proposed target group and its relevance to the programme’s theme

  • Training methodology, including interactive elements

  • Technical or logistical requirements

  • Proposed budget, including economy travel and accommodation costs in Kunming, China

Applicants should refer to the official proposal template for additional guidance.

Application Deadline

All proposals must be submitted online through the designated submission platform.

Submission Deadline: Monday, 6 April 2026

Late submissions will not be considered.

This opportunity represents a unique chance for experienced gender equality professionals to contribute to international capacity-building efforts and strengthen collaboration between Asia and Europe in advancing inclusive governance.

Professionals with strong expertise in gender equality, women’s leadership, and participatory training methodologies are encouraged to apply and play a key role in shaping the next generation of gender-responsive leaders.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Facilitator Opportunity on Gender Equality and Women’s Participation: ASEF Opens Call for Capacity-Building Programme (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Genser Energy Graduate Programme 2026 for Young African Graduates
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Young African graduates interested in launching careers in the energy sector can now apply for the Genser Energy Graduate Programme, an initiative supported by SEO Africa. The program is designed to equip talented graduates with professional skills, training, and industry experience that prepare them for careers in the energy industry. Through this programme, selected candidates receive […]

The post Apply for the Genser Energy Graduate Programme 2026 for Young African Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young African graduates interested in launching careers in the energy sector can now apply for the Genser Energy Graduate Programme, an initiative supported by SEO Africa. The program is designed to equip talented graduates with professional skills, training, and industry experience that prepare them for careers in the energy industry.

Through this programme, selected candidates receive structured training and mentorship before being placed as graduate trainees with Genser Energy Ghana, where they gain practical experience working on real energy projects.

The initiative aims to bridge the gap between academic education and the professional world by providing young graduates with the tools, networks, and experience needed to succeed in corporate careers.

About the Genser Energy Graduate Programme

The Genser Energy Graduate Programme is part of SEO Africa’s broader mission to support young African professionals by connecting them with career opportunities and professional development programs. SEO Africa has spent more than a decade helping talented students and graduates access career pathways across sectors such as finance, technology, consulting, and energy.

The programme specifically focuses on preparing graduates for careers within the energy sector, one of the most important industries driving economic growth and infrastructure development across Africa.

Participants go through a structured programme that combines technical training, professional development, and mentorship, ensuring they develop both industry knowledge and workplace skills.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Energy plays a critical role in economic development across Africa. Expanding access to reliable and sustainable energy infrastructure is essential for supporting industries, improving livelihoods, and enabling innovation.

However, building strong energy systems requires a new generation of skilled professionals capable of managing complex projects and driving innovation.

Programs like the Genser Energy Graduate Programme help address this need by developing young professionals who can contribute to Africa’s growing energy sector.

Through structured training and industry placements, the programme enables graduates to build valuable experience while contributing to projects that support energy development across the region.

For young professionals interested in engineering, technology, business, or energy-related careers, the programme offers an opportunity to gain real-world experience and build a strong foundation for future leadership.

What the Programme Offers

Successful applicants benefit from several professional development opportunities designed to accelerate their careers.

Professional Training

Participants receive world-class professional training that focuses on both technical knowledge and essential workplace skills. This training prepares graduates to transition effectively from university studies into professional roles within the energy sector.

Training sessions may include areas such as technical industry knowledge, teamwork, communication skills, and leadership development.

Mentorship and Career Support

Selected graduates are paired with experienced mentors who provide guidance throughout the programme. Mentorship plays a key role in helping participants understand industry expectations, refine their career goals, and navigate professional challenges.

This support helps young professionals develop the confidence and knowledge needed to succeed in competitive industries.

Direct Corporate Placement

After completing the training phase, participants transition into graduate trainee roles with Genser Energy Ghana, where they work within operational teams and contribute to real energy projects.

This hands-on experience allows participants to apply their academic knowledge while gaining exposure to industry practices and professional workflows.

Clear Career Pathway

The programme provides a structured pathway from academic study to full-time employment. By combining training, mentorship, and practical work experience, the programme prepares participants for long-term careers within the energy industry.

Who Should Apply

The Genser Energy Graduate Programme is designed for high-performing final-year university students and recent graduates who want to start careers in the energy sector.

Eligible candidates typically include:

  • Final-year university students preparing to graduate
  • Recent graduates who completed their degrees within the last two years
  • Candidates with strong academic performance and leadership potential
  • Applicants motivated to pursue careers in the energy industry

The programme’s rigorous selection process focuses on identifying talented graduates with strong analytical abilities, leadership potential, and a commitment to professional growth.

Application Timeline

Applications for the Genser Energy Graduate Programme are currently open.

Application Deadline: March 29, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to review the programme details and submit their applications before the deadline. You can find more details and the application portal on the official SEO Africa website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for the Genser Energy Graduate Programme 2026 for Young African Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the In Her Name Scholarships 2026 for Women from Migrant and Multicultural Backgrounds in Australia
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Women from migrant and multicultural backgrounds in Australia now have the opportunity to accelerate their professional and creative journeys through the In Her Name Scholarships 2026. The scholarship program supports emerging women leaders who are building careers, launching businesses, or developing creative projects. Each scholarship provides $10,000 in funding along with mentorship, professional networks, and […]

The post Apply for the In Her Name Scholarships 2026 for Women from Migrant and Multicultural Backgrounds in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Women from migrant and multicultural backgrounds in Australia now have the opportunity to accelerate their professional and creative journeys through the In Her Name Scholarships 2026.

The scholarship program supports emerging women leaders who are building careers, launching businesses, or developing creative projects. Each scholarship provides $10,000 in funding along with mentorship, professional networks, and national visibility to help recipients advance their ambitions.

The initiative aims to empower women from culturally diverse communities by providing financial support and access to mentorship and networks that can help transform ideas into sustainable careers or ventures.

Applicants working in business, creative industries, fashion entrepreneurship, or health-related professions are encouraged to apply.

About the In Her Name Scholarships

The In Her Name Scholarships were created to support women from migrant and multicultural backgrounds who are pursuing meaningful work and leadership opportunities across different sectors.

The program recognizes that women from culturally diverse communities often face unique barriers when building careers or businesses. By providing financial support and mentorship, the scholarships aim to create opportunities for women to expand their impact and reach their professional goals.

Beyond financial assistance, the program also offers access to mentorship, professional networks, and national exposure, helping recipients connect with leaders, organizations, and opportunities that can help them advance.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Women from migrant and multicultural backgrounds play a vital role in shaping Australia’s cultural, economic, and creative landscape. However, access to mentorship, funding, and professional networks can sometimes be limited.

Scholarship initiatives like In Her Name help address these challenges by supporting women who are building businesses, advancing their careers, or creating meaningful projects within their communities.

By investing in emerging leaders across industries such as fashion, business, creative arts, and healthcare, the program aims to promote innovation, diversity, and leadership within Australia’s professional landscape.

For women ready to take the next step in their professional journey, the In Her Name Scholarships provide both financial support and a platform for growth.

Scholarship Categories

For the 2026 cycle, the program includes four categories designed to support women working across different professional and creative fields.

Fashion Entrepreneurship

This category supports women who are building or expanding fashion-related businesses or creative ventures. Applicants may be designers, entrepreneurs, or innovators working within the fashion industry.

The scholarship can help support activities such as developing new collections, launching brands, expanding production, or building sustainable fashion businesses.

Creative Industries

The Creative Industries category supports women working in fields such as art, media, design, storytelling, or other creative disciplines.

Applicants may use the scholarship to develop creative projects, expand their portfolios, produce new work, or build sustainable creative careers.

Business

This category is designed for women entrepreneurs who are building businesses across different sectors.

The scholarship may support activities such as business expansion, product development, marketing initiatives, or strategic growth plans.

Career Accelerator (Health and Allied Health)

The Career Accelerator category supports women pursuing professional advancement in health and allied health fields.

Applicants may include professionals working in areas such as healthcare, community health services, or medical-related professions who are seeking to further develop their careers or initiatives.

What the Scholarship Provides

Recipients of the In Her Name Scholarships receive a comprehensive support package designed to help them grow their careers or ventures.

Each scholarship includes:

  • $10,000 in funding
  • Access to mentorship and professional guidance
  • Opportunities to build professional networks
  • National visibility and recognition for their work

This combination of financial support and professional development aims to help recipients take the next step in their careers, businesses, or creative projects.

Supporting Organizations

The In Her Name Scholarships are supported by several organizations committed to promoting opportunity and inclusion.

These partners include:

  • The Australian Multicultural Foundation
  • The CARLA ZAMPATTI Foundation
  • The Scanlon Foundation
  • The Verrocchi Family Foundation
  • Wendy Daoud El-Khoury

Together, these organizations support initiatives that empower culturally diverse communities and create pathways for leadership and innovation.

Application Timeline

Applications for the In Her Name Scholarships 2026 are now open. Learn more on the official website.

Application Deadline: April 22, 2026

Applicants should review the eligibility criteria and submit their applications through the official scholarship portal before the deadline.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for the In Her Name Scholarships 2026 for Women from Migrant and Multicultural Backgrounds in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Geospatial Analysis Internship at IFAD in Rome (GIS & Remote Sensing Students)
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Students and recent graduates with skills in GIS, cartography, and remote sensing now have an opportunity to gain international development experience through a Geospatial Analysis Internship with the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD). The internship is offered within the Geospatial Unit of the Office of Development Effectiveness (ODE) at IFAD. The selected intern will […]

The post Apply for the Geospatial Analysis Internship at IFAD in Rome (GIS & Remote Sensing Students) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students and recent graduates with skills in GIS, cartography, and remote sensing now have an opportunity to gain international development experience through a Geospatial Analysis Internship with the International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD).

The internship is offered within the Geospatial Unit of the Office of Development Effectiveness (ODE) at IFAD. The selected intern will support geospatial data analysis and mapping work that contributes to monitoring development projects and evaluating their impact.

This role provides a unique chance to apply geospatial analysis tools within the context of global development, agricultural programs, and environmental monitoring, while working with a specialized team inside a United Nations agency.

The internship can be completed in person in Rome, remotely, or through a hybrid arrangement, depending on the candidate’s circumstances.

About the Internship Role

The Geospatial Unit at IFAD plays an important role in helping the organization analyze geographic data to improve development planning, monitoring, and evaluation.

Geospatial analysis allows IFAD teams to better understand where projects are located, how resources are distributed, and how rural development initiatives are impacting communities.

As a Geospatial Analysis Intern, the selected candidate will contribute to several technical tasks that support IFAD’s analytical and reporting work.

Why This Internship Matters

Geospatial data has become increasingly important in international development. Organizations like IFAD rely on geographic information systems to track project locations, evaluate outcomes, and improve planning for rural development initiatives.

By supporting mapping, satellite analysis, and geospatial data management, the Geospatial Analysis Intern will help strengthen the evidence base that guides IFAD’s development programs.

For students interested in careers in GIS, environmental monitoring, remote sensing, or development analytics, this internship provides an opportunity to gain valuable experience working with a global organization.

It also allows participants to build technical skills while contributing to projects that aim to improve livelihoods and reduce rural poverty around the world.

What the Intern Will Work On

During the internship, the selected candidate will support the Geospatial Unit with a range of geospatial data and mapping activities.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Creating maps for reports, dashboards, and operational analyses
  • Processing and maintaining geospatial datasets such as project locations and administrative boundaries
  • Supporting satellite imagery analysis
  • Contributing to geospatial workflows using tools like QGIS, ArcGIS, Google Earth Engine, Python, or R

These activities will help the intern gain practical experience working with geospatial tools in a real-world development context.

Interns will also gain exposure to how geospatial data is used in monitoring and evaluating development projects, which is an important part of IFAD’s mission.

Academic Background and Skills Required

The internship is designed for students and recent graduates with a strong interest in geospatial analysis and geographic information systems.

Applicants should have an academic background in areas such as:

  • Geospatial Sciences
  • Geography
  • Remote Sensing
  • Environmental Science
  • Geoinformatics
  • Or other related fields

Candidates should also have practical experience using GIS software, particularly tools such as QGIS or ArcGIS.

A solid understanding of cartographic principles is also expected, including knowledge of coordinate systems, projections, symbology, and scale.

Because the internship involves working with satellite data, experience with remote sensing datasets is also considered important.

Applicants must also demonstrate excellent proficiency in English, which is the working language used by IFAD teams.

Additional Skills That Are Considered an Advantage

While not mandatory, several additional skills can strengthen an application.

These include:

  • Experience using Python or R for geospatial analysis
  • Familiarity with Google Earth Engine
  • Interest in international development, agriculture, climate research, or environmental monitoring

These additional skills can help candidates contribute more effectively to IFAD’s analytical workflows.

Internship Location and Duration

The Geospatial Analysis Internship is designed to be flexible and accessible for students and early-career professionals.

Key details include:

  • Location: Rome, Italy (with options for in-person, remote, or hybrid work)
  • Duration: 6 months
  • Working hours: Flexible, with part-time or full-time arrangements available

This flexibility allows students to gain international experience while balancing academic commitments.

Application Deadline

The opportunity is available through the IFAD Internship Programme portal, and applicants should submit their applications through the official system. To access the system you must create a profile first.

Because internship opportunities may close once a suitable candidate is identified, interested applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for the Geospatial Analysis Internship at IFAD in Rome (GIS & Remote Sensing Students) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Citizen Security Research Initiative Call for Proposals 2026
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Researchers working on crime prevention, justice systems, and public safety now have an opportunity to receive funding through the Citizen Security Research Initiative (CSRI) Call for Proposals, launched by Innovations for Poverty Action (IPA). The initiative supports rigorous research designed to generate evidence that can inform policies and programs aimed at building safer communities and […]

The post Apply for the Citizen Security Research Initiative Call for Proposals 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers working on crime prevention, justice systems, and public safety now have an opportunity to receive funding through the Citizen Security Research Initiative (CSRI) Call for Proposals, launched by Innovations for Poverty Action (IPA).

The initiative supports rigorous research designed to generate evidence that can inform policies and programs aimed at building safer communities and strengthening justice and law enforcement systems. The program is funded by the U.S. Department of State’s Bureau of International Narcotics and Law Enforcement Affairs (INL).

Through this call, researchers are invited to submit proposals for projects that investigate innovative approaches to reducing crime and violence, improving justice systems, and strengthening institutional responses to public safety challenges.

The initiative seeks research that produces actionable evidence to guide policymakers, practitioners, and organizations working in the citizen security and justice sector.

About the Citizen Security Research Initiative

The Citizen Security Research Initiative (CSRI) aims to support high-quality research that addresses key challenges related to crime, violence, and justice systems.

Projects supported through the initiative are expected to generate evidence that can help governments, international organizations, and civil society design more effective policies and programs.

The initiative supports a range of research approaches, including:

  • Rigorous impact evaluations
  • Pilot studies and exploratory research
  • Infrastructure and public goods projects
  • Foundational and contextual research

Why This Opportunity Matters

Crime, violence, and weak justice systems continue to affect communities around the world. Policymakers and practitioners often face significant challenges when designing effective strategies to address these issues due to limited evidence about what works.

Research initiatives like CSRI help fill this gap by supporting studies that produce reliable and policy-relevant evidence.

By funding innovative research projects and impact evaluations, the initiative aims to help governments and organizations develop more effective approaches to crime prevention, law enforcement, and justice reform.

The research supported through this initiative has the potential to inform policies that improve public safety, strengthen institutions, and contribute to safer communities.

Research Priority Areas

The call focuses on several priority areas where more evidence is needed to inform effective policy and program design.

Researchers are encouraged to submit proposals addressing topics such as:

Countering Organized Crime

Projects may examine strategies for disrupting organized criminal networks, improving law enforcement responses, or strengthening institutional coordination to address organized crime.

Combating Drug Trafficking and Production

The initiative supports research exploring how policies, enforcement strategies, and prevention programs can reduce drug trafficking and production.

Preventing Crime and Violence

Another priority area is identifying interventions that reduce crime and violence within communities. This may include research on prevention programs, community-based initiatives, or behavioral interventions.

Strengthening Law Enforcement and Justice Systems

Projects may also focus on improving criminal justice institutions, strengthening policing strategies, and promoting more effective justice system responses to crime and violence.

The initiative particularly encourages proposals that explore innovative interventions and generate evidence where little currently exists.

Types of Projects Supported

The CSRI call supports multiple types of research projects depending on the scope and objectives of the proposed study.

Possible project types include:

Impact Evaluations
Rigorous studies designed to evaluate the effectiveness of policies or interventions related to citizen security.

Pilot Studies
Small-scale projects that test innovative approaches before larger-scale implementation.

Research Infrastructure Projects
Initiatives that build datasets, tools, or resources that can support future research in the citizen security field.

Foundational and Contextual Research
Studies that help researchers better understand the social, political, and institutional factors that influence crime, violence, and justice systems.

These project types allow researchers to explore new ideas while also building the evidence base needed to improve public safety strategies.

Who Can Apply

The Citizen Security Research Initiative is open to researchers from around the world, but applications must be submitted through a recognized research institution.

Eligible applicants typically include researchers affiliated with:

  • Universities and academic institutions
  • Research organizations or think tanks
  • Policy research institutes or nonprofit research centers

Applicants are generally expected to have strong research experience, and many successful proposals are led by PhD holders or doctoral researchers with expertise in impact evaluation or policy research.

Importantly, the grant funding is awarded to the host institution managing the project, rather than directly to individuals. Researchers are also encouraged to collaborate with implementation partners such as NGOs, governments, or civil society organizations to test or evaluate interventions in real-world settings.

Application Timeline & Process

Proposals typically include:

  • A narrative proposal describing the research project
  • A project budget
  • Supporting documentation such as letters of support

Applicants are encouraged to review the official application instructions and proposal guidelines carefully before submitting their proposals. Learn more on the official call for proposals page.

Researchers interested in applying must submit their proposals before:

Application Deadline: April 15, 2026

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply for the Citizen Security Research Initiative Call for Proposals 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Funding Opportunity 2026: Engaged Democracy Initiative Response Hub Grants for Civic Organizations
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Civil society organizations and grassroots movements across the Western Balkans now have a new opportunity to access flexible funding through the Response Hub Grants under the European Fund for the Balkans Engaged Democracy Initiative (EDI). Designed to respond to the fast-changing realities faced by civic actors, the Response Hub offers rapid, adaptable financial support to […]

The post Funding Opportunity 2026: Engaged Democracy Initiative Response Hub Grants for Civic Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Civil society organizations and grassroots movements across the Western Balkans now have a new opportunity to access flexible funding through the Response Hub Grants under the European Fund for the Balkans Engaged Democracy Initiative (EDI).

Designed to respond to the fast-changing realities faced by civic actors, the Response Hub offers rapid, adaptable financial support to organizations, initiatives, media outlets, and activists working to protect democratic values and public interests.

Whether responding to a sudden social crisis, experimenting with innovative civic solutions, or maintaining organizational stability during difficult times, the Response Hub provides resources that move “at the speed of real life.”

About the Engaged Democracy Initiative Response Hub

The Response Hub is a flexible funding mechanism created by the European Fund for the Balkans through the Engaged Democracy Initiative.

The program provides micro-financial and non-financial support to civic groups and initiatives in the Western Balkans that are working to strengthen democratic processes, protect human rights, and promote accountable governance.

Supported projects must align with one of the EDI thematic focus areas:

  • Human Rights

  • Environment

  • Media

  • Public Spaces

  • Decent Work

Through this initiative, the fund aims to empower organizations that play a critical role in defending democratic institutions and promoting the public interest.

Grant Categories Available

Applicants can choose from five grant categories, each designed to address different needs within the civic sector.

1. Social Urgency Grants (Up to €7,000)

Social Urgency Grants support rapid-response civic actions during critical social, political, or environmental moments.

These grants help organizations react quickly to events such as:

  • Emergency advocacy campaigns

  • Protests against harmful developments

  • Community mobilization during local crises

  • Rapid policy response initiatives

By providing fast funding, the program ensures civic voices remain active during moments when timely action is crucial.

2. Organizational Urgency Grants (Up to €700)

Many grassroots organizations face operational challenges due to unexpected financial gaps. Organizational Urgency Grants help cover essential operational costs, allowing organizations to remain active.

Eligible expenses may include:

  • Electricity

  • Internet services

  • Rent

  • Heating or utilities

These grants act as a lifeline for organizations experiencing short-term financial pressure.

Note: Organizations may receive this grant only once per calendar year, and a limited number of awards are available.

3. Innovation Grants (Up to €5,000)

Innovation Grants support experimentation and creative problem-solving within the civic sector.

These grants provide resources for organizations to:

  • Pilot new civic engagement approaches

  • Test digital transparency tools

  • Develop innovative communication strategies

  • Experiment with new community outreach models

Projects can run for up to six months, encouraging learning, testing, and iteration.

4. Travel Grants (Up to €1,500)

Travel Grants enable civic actors to connect with regional and international networks, strengthening collaboration and knowledge exchange.

Funds may cover:

  • Travel costs

  • Accommodation

  • Participation fees

Eligible activities include:

  • International conferences

  • Advocacy meetings in regional capitals or Brussels

  • Specialized training workshops

These grants help activists build regional solidarity and strategic partnerships.

5. Organizational Development Grants (Up to €2,000)

Organizational Development Grants help civic groups strengthen their institutional capacity and long-term sustainability.

Supported activities may include:

  • Strategic planning sessions

  • Development of organizational bylaws

  • Financial management training

  • Internal governance improvements

The goal is to enhance the organizational backbone needed to manage larger projects and challenges.

Eligibility Criteria

The program is open to applicants from the Western Balkans Six (WB6) countries:

  • Albania

  • Bosnia and Herzegovina

  • Kosovo

  • Montenegro

  • North Macedonia

  • Serbia

Eligible Applicants

  • Registered non-governmental organizations (NGOs)

  • Informal movements or grassroots initiatives

  • Regional civil society networks

Informal groups may apply through:

  • A Fiscal Host (a registered NGO), or

  • Consultancy contracts.

Financial Guidelines

Applicants should consider the following financial conditions:

  • Grant amounts are gross, meaning all applicable taxes and bank fees must be included in the requested budget.

  • Informal groups using a fiscal host may allocate up to 7% of the total grant to cover administrative and accounting costs.

Application Language

Applications are accepted in English. However, applicants may also submit proposals in their local language and translate them using an online tool.

The program emphasizes accessibility and confirms that grammar will not affect evaluation.

Click Here, for more details about  the application.

Rapid Review and Decision Process

The Response Hub is designed to provide quick and efficient decisions.

  • Rolling Applications: Submissions are accepted throughout the year.

  • Final Deadline: September 3, 2026

Decision Timeline

  • Social Urgency Grants: Decision within 48 business hours

  • All other grant categories: Decision within 15 days

All applications are reviewed by an Independent Selection Committee.

Why This Grant Matters

The Engaged Democracy Initiative Response Hub offers something many civic funding programs lack: speed, flexibility, and responsiveness.

By supporting grassroots actors during urgent moments, experimentation phases, and organizational challenges, the initiative helps strengthen democratic participation and civic resilience across the Western Balkans.

Organizations and movements working on human rights, environmental protection, media freedom, public space advocacy, and labor rights are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this opportunity.

Discover more Grant Opportunities Here

The post Funding Opportunity 2026: Engaged Democracy Initiative Response Hub Grants for Civic Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship 2026: Apply to Gain First-Hand Experience in U.S. Federal Policymaking
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship 2026, an exceptional opportunity for scientists and researchers to gain hands-on experience in the United States federal policymaking process. The fellowship provides participants with the chance to apply their scientific knowledge and analytical expertise to real-world policy challenges while working closely with policymakers […]

The post John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship 2026: Apply to Gain First-Hand Experience in U.S. Federal Policymaking appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship 2026, an exceptional opportunity for scientists and researchers to gain hands-on experience in the United States federal policymaking process. The fellowship provides participants with the chance to apply their scientific knowledge and analytical expertise to real-world policy challenges while working closely with policymakers in Washington, D.C.

The program is designed to bridge the gap between science and public policy by placing fellows directly within the legislative or executive branches of the U.S. government. During the fellowship, participants contribute their expertise to policy discussions and decisions that shape national and global priorities.

The John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship is administered by the American Astronomical Society, a leading professional organization dedicated to advancing astronomy and related sciences. The fellowship is named in honor of John N. Bahcall, a renowned astrophysicist known for his contributions to science and his dedication to connecting scientific research with public policy.

About the John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship

The fellowship was created to enable scientists and researchers to understand the policymaking process from the inside. Fellows are placed in offices within the United States government, where they assist with policy research, analysis, and development.

Participants work alongside policymakers and staff members to address pressing issues related to science, technology, and public policy. Fellows gain insight into how policy decisions are made and how scientific expertise can inform those decisions.

By immersing scientists in the policymaking environment, the fellowship strengthens the relationship between the scientific community and government institutions.

Fellowship Experience

Fellows selected for the program spend a year working in the U.S. federal government, contributing their expertise to policy discussions and legislative processes.

The fellowship provides a unique opportunity to observe how science intersects with policy at the national level. Fellows may contribute to:

  • Policy research and analysis

  • Legislative briefings and reports

  • Scientific advisory work

  • Policy discussions related to science, technology, and innovation

Through this experience, fellows develop a deeper understanding of government processes while strengthening their ability to communicate scientific concepts to policymakers and stakeholders.

Professional Development Opportunities

In addition to hands-on policy work, fellows gain valuable professional development experience.

Participants expand their networks by connecting with policymakers, scientists, and policy experts from across the United States government and research community.

The fellowship also enhances participants’ leadership and communication skills by requiring them to translate complex scientific ideas into practical policy insights.

These experiences prepare fellows for future careers at the intersection of science, policy, and public leadership.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Scientific knowledge plays a critical role in shaping policies that address global challenges such as climate change, technological innovation, energy security, and space exploration.

However, scientists are often underrepresented in policymaking processes. The John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship aims to address this gap by placing scientists directly in policymaking environments where their expertise can inform important decisions.

By empowering scientists to contribute to policy development, the fellowship strengthens evidence-based policymaking and fosters collaboration between the scientific and policy communities.

Benefits of the Fellowship

The John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship offers several key benefits for selected fellows.

Participants gain first-hand experience working within the U.S. federal government, providing valuable insight into legislative and executive decision-making processes.

The fellowship also provides professional development opportunities that enhance leadership, communication, and policy analysis skills.

In addition, fellows join a network of professionals working at the intersection of science and policy, opening doors to future opportunities in government, academia, and research institutions.

Application Deadline

Applications will be reviewed as they are received, with preference given to applications submitted by April 15, 2026.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to increase their chances of being considered during the priority review period.

How to Apply

Applicants interested in the fellowship should prepare and submit their application through the official program portal.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning submissions are evaluated as they are received. Candidates are therefore encouraged to apply as early as possible.

For more information about eligibility requirements, application guidelines, and submission instructions, visit the official application page.

To get Access to more Fellowship opportunities, visit OFY here.

The post John N. Bahcall Public Policy Fellowship 2026: Apply to Gain First-Hand Experience in U.S. Federal Policymaking appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ACET Economist Job Opportunity: Join the Youth Employment and Skills Team at the African Center for Economic Transformation
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET) is inviting applications for the position of Economist – Youth Employment and Skills (YES), offering professionals the opportunity to contribute to research and policy initiatives aimed at transforming Africa’s economies and improving employment outcomes for young people. This role provides a unique opportunity to work with leading economists […]

The post ACET Economist Job Opportunity: Join the Youth Employment and Skills Team at the African Center for Economic Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET) is inviting applications for the position of Economist – Youth Employment and Skills (YES), offering professionals the opportunity to contribute to research and policy initiatives aimed at transforming Africa’s economies and improving employment outcomes for young people.

This role provides a unique opportunity to work with leading economists and policy experts, including the Youth Employment and Skills team led by Mona Iddrisu, who are actively shaping policies and research on labor markets, education systems, and youth employment across Africa.

Based in Accra, Ghana, with remote work flexibility, this position allows candidates to work at one of Africa’s most respected policy institutes dedicated to driving sustainable economic transformation on the continent.

About the African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET)

The African Center for Economic Transformation is a leading pan-African economic policy institute that works to promote long-term economic growth and development across Africa. The organization produces research, provides policy advice, and collaborates with governments, private sector actors, and development partners to support inclusive economic transformation.

Founded in 2008, ACET focuses on developing African-led solutions to the continent’s economic challenges. Its work spans several key policy areas including economic management and governance, private sector development, regional integration, gender equality, and youth employment.

Through its research and advisory services, ACET has supported numerous African governments and institutions in designing and implementing policies that promote sustainable growth and improved livelihoods.

About the Youth Employment and Skills (YES) Program

The Youth Employment and Skills program at ACET focuses on addressing one of Africa’s most pressing development challenges: the growing gap between education systems and labor market needs.

The program conducts research and policy analysis to help governments identify solutions that improve employment outcomes for young people. Its work includes analyzing labor market trends, evaluating education and training systems, and identifying sectors with strong job creation potential.

The YES program also engages youth organizations, policymakers, and development partners in dialogue and collaboration to develop evidence-based policies that strengthen Africa’s workforce.

Role Overview: Economist – Youth Employment and Skills

The Economist will work closely with the Head of the YES program and other researchers to conduct economic research and policy analysis related to youth employment, education systems, and labor market dynamics across sub-Saharan Africa.

The successful candidate will contribute to ACET’s mission by producing research, analyzing economic trends, and supporting projects that influence policy reforms aimed at improving employment opportunities for young people.

This role is ideal for professionals with strong analytical skills and a passion for economic research, policy development, and development economics.

Key Responsibilities

The Economist will undertake several responsibilities within the Youth Employment and Skills team.

The role involves conducting research and analytical work on economic issues affecting youth employment, education systems, and labor markets across Africa.

The selected candidate will gather and analyze datasets, develop research databases, and support data-driven policy analysis.

The economist will also prepare reports, research publications, and presentations that communicate findings to policymakers, governments, and development partners.

Another key responsibility includes supporting collaborative research initiatives and contributing technical expertise to projects within the YES program.

In addition, the economist may serve as a spokesperson representing ACET in policy discussions, conferences, and engagements with stakeholders working on economic transformation and youth development.

Required Qualifications and Experience

Applicants interested in the Economist position should meet the following requirements.

Candidates must possess a Master’s degree in Economics or Statistics, preferably with specialization in areas such as labor economics, development economics, macroeconomics, or education policy.

Applicants should have at least five years of relevant professional experience, particularly in research or policy analysis related to labor markets, education systems, or economic development.

Strong quantitative and analytical skills are essential for the role, including experience with statistical and econometric analysis.

Candidates are expected to demonstrate proficiency in analytical tools such as STATA, EViews, R, or SAS, along with strong knowledge of Microsoft Office tools.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills are also required, as the role involves translating complex research findings into accessible insights for policymakers and stakeholders.

Fluency in English is required, while knowledge of French will be considered an advantage.

Why Work at ACET

Working at ACET provides professionals with the opportunity to contribute to impactful research and policy work that influences economic development across Africa.

Employees collaborate with leading economists, researchers, and development experts, gaining exposure to major policy discussions shaping Africa’s future.

The organization is also known for its collaborative work environment and commitment to advancing African-led solutions to development challenges.

Joining the Youth Employment and Skills team offers the chance to contribute directly to solutions that address youth unemployment, one of the continent’s most pressing economic issues.

Application Deadline

Applicants are advised to consult the official vacancy page for the latest information regarding the application deadline and submission requirements.

Early applications are strongly encouraged as recruitment processes may close once suitable candidates are identified.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should prepare a CV and a cover letter explaining why they are suitable for the position.

Applications should be submitted via email to the address provided on the official vacancy page.

For more details about the role and application requirements, visit the official opportunity page.

For more Job opportunities, Click here.

The post ACET Economist Job Opportunity: Join the Youth Employment and Skills Team at the African Center for Economic Transformation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
